what_it_does: It is a session plan and ordered reading list for unifying the Vi system: it states philosophy and design principles, lists files to read in sequence, enumerates prioritized work items, identifies additional surfaces to wire, and lists guardrails for the next implementation pass.
observations: The document opens with philosophy and design principles before presenting any tasks.
It provides an 18-item numbered reading list that must be read fully and in order.

Work is sequenced as #1 close the learning-to-generation loop, #2 add a deterministic 'here is what I can make' moment, #3 add a 'Vi learned from this' inline indicator, then the larger floating-Vi unification.
It explicitly forbids grep/search-based research and demands full-file reading as a non-negotiable practice.
It names drift — divergent colors, spacing, parallel stores, inconsistent behavior — as the central risk of partial work.
Guardrails include tokens/registries only, reusable components, TDD in run_script, schema mirroring, and verification before forking.
No code symbols are present in the file.
standouts: The 'RESEARCH BY READING, NOT SEARCHING' directive is called out as non-negotiable and tied to past failures.
Unification is framed as the product itself, not a background refactor or feature.
The floating Vi assistant is identified as the concentrated place where unified awareness must live.
conventions: Uses a numbered, ordered checklist structure.
Separates concerns into Philosophy, Read-This-First, The Work, Candidates to Wire, and Guardrails.
Refers to modules with an MS_ prefix (MS_TOKENS, MS_KINDS, MS_RESOLVE, etc.).
Demands single-source registries, reusable shared components, and loud failure instead of silent fallbacks.
Requires schema mirroring in db/schema.sql for any new persisted concept.
concerns: The reading list is large and may exceed practical context budgets in a single session.
Work items are high-level and do not include explicit acceptance criteria or metrics.
The instruction to 'work in big increments' sits beside 'verify each,' which could be interpreted inconsistently.
Many module abbreviations are used without defining them in this file, assuming prior system knowledge.
notes: A session plan that makes reading the whole system before coding a non-negotiable guardrail, then maps the work of wiring feedback, memory, and context into the floating Vi assistant.
questions: What is the exact threshold or definition of 'canSynthesize' for the deterministic moment?
How is 'scope' determined when querying MS_FEEDBACK.signalsFor?
What does 'work in big increments' mean in practice given the verify-each requirement?
Which surfaces currently violate the no-search or no-parallel-store rules?
What is the current implementation state of the floating Vi's context builder?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it is a plan and reading list for the next session, and it delivers exactly that.
apparent_themes: Unified intelligence over a pile of features
Single source of truth across registries
Loud failure and no silent fallbacks
Contextual awareness from project brief, memory, feedback, and launch location
Anti-drift through reading and shared components
intention_signals: It is trying to keep the Vi system from fragmenting into disconnected screens by forcing every surface to read from and write to one shared substrate, and by making the floating assistant the living expression of project context, memory, and feedback.
novelty: It treats unification itself as the primary product objective and includes an explicit, forceful anti-search research clause.
contribution: It captures the unification intent, the ordered reading map, the prioritized work sequence, and the guardrails that future agents must follow when extending the Vi substrate.
summary_for_embedding: UNIFICATION_PLAN.md is a session plan for the Counterpart Vi project that prescribes philosophy, an ordered reading list, sequenced work items, and guardrails to unify registries, memory, feedback, and the floating Vi assistant.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to guide the next development session toward a single context-aware intelligence by reading the whole surface area before making changes.
